lines.gurmukhi: ਜੋ ਗੁਰੁ ਗੋਪੇ ਆਪਣਾ ਸੁ ਭਲਾ ਨਾਹੀ ਪੰਚਹੁ ਓਨਿ ਲਾਹਾ ਮੂਲੁ ਸਭੁ ਗਵਾਇਆ ॥
lines.translit: jo gur gope aapanaa su bhalaa naahee panchahu on laahaa mool sabh gavaaeaa |
lines.english: O chosen people, O self-elect, one who does not publicly affirm his Guru is not a good person; he loses all his profits and capital.

lines.gurmukhi: ਪਹਿਲਾ ਆਗਮੁ ਨਿਗਮੁ ਨਾਨਕੁ ਆਖਿ ਸੁਣਾਏ ਪੂਰੇ ਗੁਰ ਕਾ ਬਚਨੁ ਉਪਰਿ ਆਇਆ ॥
lines.translit: pahilaa aagam nigam naanak aakh sunaae poore gur kaa bachan upar aaeaa |
lines.english: People used to chant and recite the Shaastras and the Vedas, O Nanak, but now the Words of the Perfect Guru have come to be the most exalted of all.

lines.gurmukhi: ਗੁਰਸਿਖਾ ਵਡਿਆਈ ਭਾਵੈ ਗੁਰ ਪੂਰੇ ਕੀ ਮਨਮੁਖਾ ਓਹ ਵੇਲਾ ਹਥਿ ਨ ਆਇਆ ॥੨॥
lines.translit: gurasikhaa vaddiaaee bhaavai gur poore kee manamukhaa oeh velaa hath na aaeaa |2|
lines.english: The glorious greatness of the Perfect Guru is pleasing to the GurSikh; the self-willed manmukhs have lost this opportunity. ||2||

lines.gurmukhi: ਪਉੜੀ ॥
lines.translit: paurree |
lines.english: Pauree:

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਚੁ ਸਚਾ ਸਭ ਦੂ ਵਡਾ ਹੈ ਸੋ ਲਏ ਜਿਸੁ ਸਤਿਗੁਰੁ ਟਿਕੇ ॥
lines.translit: sach sachaa sabh doo vaddaa hai so le jis satigur ttike |
lines.english: The True Lord is truly the greatest of all; he alone obtains Him, who is anointed by the Guru.

lines.gurmukhi: ਸੋ ਸਤਿਗੁਰੁ ਜਿ ਸਚੁ ਧਿਆਇਦਾ ਸਚੁ ਸਚਾ ਸਤਿਗੁਰੁ ਇਕੇ ॥
lines.translit: so satigur ji sach dhiaaeidaa sach sachaa satigur ike |
lines.english: He is the True Guru, who meditates on the True Lord. The True Lord and the True Guru are truly One.

lines.gurmukhi: ਸੋਈ ਸਤਿਗੁਰੁ ਪੁਰਖੁ ਹੈ ਜਿਨਿ ਪੰਜੇ ਦੂਤ ਕੀਤੇ ਵਸਿ ਛਿਕੇ ॥
lines.translit: soee satigur purakh hai jin panje doot keete vas chhike |
lines.english: He is the True Guru, the Primal Being, who has totally conquered his five passions.

lines.gurmukhi: ਜਿ ਬਿਨੁ ਸਤਿਗੁਰ ਸੇਵੇ ਆਪੁ ਗਣਾਇਦੇ ਤਿਨ ਅੰਦਰਿ ਕੂੜੁ ਫਿਟੁ ਫਿਟੁ ਮੁਹ ਫਿਕੇ ॥
lines.translit: ji bin satigur seve aap ganaaeide tin andar koorr fitt fitt muh fike |
lines.english: One who does not serve the True Guru, and who praises himself, is filled with falsehood within. Cursed, cursed is his ugly face.

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਲੋਕ ਮਃ ੪ ॥
lines.translit: salok mahalaa 4 |
lines.english: Salok, Fourth Mehl:

lines.gurmukhi: ਹਰਿ ਪ੍ਰਭ ਕਾ ਸਭੁ ਖੇਤੁ ਹੈ ਹਰਿ ਆਪਿ ਕਿਰਸਾਣੀ ਲਾਇਆ ॥
lines.translit: har prabh kaa sabh khet hai har aap kirasaanee laaeaa |
lines.english: Everyone is the field of the Lord God; the Lord Himself cultivates this field.

lines.gurmukhi: ਓਇ ਬੋਲੇ ਕਿਸੈ ਨ ਭਾਵਨੀ ਮੁਹ ਕਾਲੇ ਸਤਿਗੁਰ ਤੇ ਚੁਕੇ ॥੮॥
lines.translit: oe bole kisai na bhaavanee muh kaale satigur te chuke |8|
lines.english: His words are not pleasing to anyone; his face is blackened, and he is separated from the True Guru. ||8||

lines.gurmukhi: ਗੁਰਮੁਖਿ ਬਖਸਿ ਜਮਾਈਅਨੁ ਮਨਮੁਖੀ ਮੂਲੁ ਗਵਾਇਆ ॥
lines.translit: guramukh bakhas jamaaeean manamukhee mool gavaaeaa |
lines.english: The Gurmukh grows the crop of forgiveness, while the self-willed manmukh loses even his roots.

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਭੁ ਕੋ ਬੀਜੇ ਆਪਣੇ ਭਲੇ ਨੋ ਹਰਿ ਭਾਵੈ ਸੋ ਖੇਤੁ ਜਮਾਇਆ ॥
lines.translit: sabh ko beeje aapane bhale no har bhaavai so khet jamaaeaa |
lines.english: They all plant for their own good, but the Lord causes to grow only that field with which He is pleased.

lines.gurmukhi: ਗੁਰਸਿਖੀ ਹਰਿ ਅੰਮ੍ਰਿਤੁ ਬੀਜਿਆ ਹਰਿ ਅੰਮ੍ਰਿਤ ਨਾਮੁ ਫਲੁ ਅੰਮ੍ਰਿਤੁ ਪਾਇਆ ॥
lines.translit: gurasikhee har amrit beejiaa har amrit naam fal amrit paaeaa |
lines.english: The GurSikh plants the seed of the Lord's Ambrosial Nectar, and obtains the Lord's Ambrosial Naam as his Ambrosial Fruit.

lines.gurmukhi: ਜਮੁ ਚੂਹਾ ਕਿਰਸ ਨਿਤ ਕੁਰਕਦਾ ਹਰਿ ਕਰਤੈ ਮਾਰਿ ਕਢਾਇਆ ॥
lines.translit: jam choohaa kiras nit kurakadaa har karatai maar kadtaaeaa |
lines.english: The mouse of Death is continually gnawing away at the crop, but the Creator Lord has beaten it off and driven it away.

lines.gurmukhi: ਕਿਰਸਾਣੀ ਜੰਮੀ ਭਾਉ ਕਰਿ ਹਰਿ ਬੋਹਲ ਬਖਸ ਜਮਾਇਆ ॥
lines.translit: kirasaanee jamee bhaau kar har bohal bakhas jamaaeaa |
lines.english: The farm was successful, by the Love of the Lord, and the crop was produced by God's Grace.

lines.gurmukhi: ਤਿਨ ਕਾ ਕਾੜਾ ਅੰਦੇਸਾ ਸਭੁ ਲਾਹਿਓਨੁ ਜਿਨੀ ਸਤਿਗੁਰੁ ਪੁਰਖੁ ਧਿਆਇਆ ॥
lines.translit: tin kaa kaarraa andesaa sabh laahion jinee satigur purakh dhiaaeaa |
lines.english: He has removed all the burning and anxiety of those, who have meditated on the True Guru, the Primal Being.

lines.translit: mahalaa 4 |
lines.english: Fourth Mehl:

lines.gurmukhi: ਜਨ ਨਾਨਕ ਨਾਮੁ ਅਰਾਧਿਆ ਆਪਿ ਤਰਿਆ ਸਭੁ ਜਗਤੁ ਤਰਾਇਆ ॥੧॥
lines.translit: jan naanak naam araadhiaa aap tariaa sabh jagat taraaeaa |1|
lines.english: O servant Nanak, one who worships and adores the Naam, the Name of the Lord, swims across, and saves the whole world as well. ||1||

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਾਰਾ ਦਿਨੁ ਲਾਲਚਿ ਅਟਿਆ ਮਨਮੁਖਿ ਹੋਰੇ ਗਲਾ ॥
lines.translit: saaraa din laalach attiaa manamukh hore galaa |
lines.english: The self-willed manmukh is occupied with greed all day long, although he may claim otherwise.

lines.gurmukhi: ਰਾਤੀ ਊਘੈ ਦਬਿਆ ਨਵੇ ਸੋਤ ਸਭਿ ਢਿਲਾ ॥
lines.translit: raatee aooghai dabiaa nave sot sabh dtilaa |
lines.english: At night, he is overcome by fatigue, and all his nine holes are weakened.

lines.gurmukhi: ਮਨਮੁਖਾ ਦੈ ਸਿਰਿ ਜੋਰਾ ਅਮਰੁ ਹੈ ਨਿਤ ਦੇਵਹਿ ਭਲਾ ॥
lines.translit: manamukhaa dai sir joraa amar hai nit deveh bhalaa |
lines.english: Over the head of the manmukh is the order of the woman; to her, he ever holds out his promises of goodness.

lines.gurmukhi: ਕਾਮਿ ਵਿਆਪੇ ਕੁਸੁਧ ਨਰ ਸੇ ਜੋਰਾ ਪੁਛਿ ਚਲਾ ॥
lines.translit: kaam viaape kusudh nar se joraa puchh chalaa |
lines.english: Those impure men are engrossed in sexual desire; they consult their women and walk accordingly.

lines.gurmukhi: ਜੋਰਾ ਦਾ ਆਖਿਆ ਪੁਰਖ ਕਮਾਵਦੇ ਸੇ ਅਪਵਿਤ ਅਮੇਧ ਖਲਾ ॥
lines.translit: joraa daa aakhiaa purakh kamaavade se apavit amedh khalaa |
lines.english: Those men who act according to the orders of women are impure, filthy and foolish.

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਤਿਗੁਰ ਕੈ ਆਖਿਐ ਜੋ ਚਲੈ ਸੋ ਸਤਿ ਪੁਰਖੁ ਭਲ ਭਲਾ ॥
lines.translit: satigur kai aakhiai jo chalai so sat purakh bhal bhalaa |
lines.english: One who walks as the True Guru tells him to, is the true man, the best of the best.

lines.gurmukhi: ਜੋਰਾ ਪੁਰਖ ਸਭਿ ਆਪਿ ਉਪਾਇਅਨੁ ਹਰਿ ਖੇਲ ਸਭਿ ਖਿਲਾ ॥
lines.translit: joraa purakh sabh aap upaaeian har khel sabh khilaa |
lines.english: He Himself created all women and men; the Lord Himself plays every play.

lines.gurmukhi: ਤੂ ਵੇਪਰਵਾਹੁ ਅਥਾਹੁ ਹੈ ਅਤੁਲੁ ਕਿਉ ਤੁਲੀਐ ॥
lines.translit: too veparavaahu athaahu hai atul kiau tuleeai |
lines.english: You are carefree, unfathomable and immeasurable; how can You be measured?

lines.gurmukhi: ਪਉੜੀ ॥
lines.translit: paurree |
lines.english: Pauree:

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਭ ਤੇਰੀ ਬਣਤ ਬਣਾਵਣੀ ਨਾਨਕ ਭਲ ਭਲਾ ॥੨॥
lines.translit: sabh teree banat banaavanee naanak bhal bhalaa |2|
lines.english: You created the entire creation; O Nanak, it is the best of the best. ||2||

lines.gurmukhi: ਸੇ ਵਡਭਾਗੀ ਜਿ ਤੁਧੁ ਧਿਆਇਦੇ ਜਿਨ ਸਤਿਗੁਰੁ ਮਿਲੀਐ ॥
lines.translit: se vaddabhaagee ji tudh dhiaaeide jin satigur mileeai |
lines.english: Those who have met the True Guru and who meditate on You are very fortunate.

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਤਿਗੁਰ ਕੀ ਬਾਣੀ ਸਤਿ ਸਰੂਪੁ ਹੈ ਗੁਰਬਾਣੀ ਬਣੀਐ ॥
lines.translit: satigur kee baanee sat saroop hai gurabaanee baneeai |
lines.english: The Word of the True Guru's Bani is the embodiment of Truth; through Gurbani, one becomes perfect.

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਤਿਗੁਰ ਕੀ ਰੀਸੈ ਹੋਰਿ ਕਚੁ ਪਿਚੁ ਬੋਲਦੇ ਸੇ ਕੂੜਿਆਰ ਕੂੜੇ ਝੜਿ ਪੜੀਐ ॥
lines.translit: satigur kee reesai hor kach pich bolade se koorriaar koorre jharr parreeai |
lines.english: Jealously emulating the True Guru, some others may speak of good and bad, but the false are destroyed by their falsehood.

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਤਿਗੁਰ ਕੀ ਸੇਵਾ ਨਿਰਮਲੀ ਨਿਰਮਲ ਜਨੁ ਹੋਇ ਸੁ ਸੇਵਾ ਘਾਲੇ ॥
lines.translit: satigur kee sevaa niramalee niramal jan hoe su sevaa ghaale |
lines.english: Service to the True Guru is immaculate and pure; those humble beings who are pure perform this service.

lines.gurmukhi: ਓਨੑਾ ਅੰਦਰਿ ਹੋਰੁ ਮੁਖਿ ਹੋਰੁ ਹੈ ਬਿਖੁ ਮਾਇਆ ਨੋ ਝਖਿ ਮਰਦੇ ਕੜੀਐ ॥੯॥
lines.translit: onaa andar hor mukh hor hai bikh maaeaa no jhakh marade karreeai |9|
lines.english: Deep within them is one thing, and in their mouths is another; they suck in the poison of Maya, and then they painfully waste away. ||9||

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਲੋਕ ਮਃ ੪ ॥
lines.translit: salok mahalaa 4 |
lines.english: Salok, Fourth Mehl:

lines.gurmukhi: ਜਿਨ ਅੰਦਰਿ ਕਪਟੁ ਵਿਕਾਰੁ ਝੂਠੁ ਓਇ ਆਪੇ ਸਚੈ ਵਖਿ ਕਢੇ ਜਜਮਾਲੇ ॥
lines.translit: jin andar kapatt vikaar jhootth oe aape sachai vakh kadte jajamaale |
lines.english: Those who have deceit, corruption and falsehood within - the True Lord Himself casts them out like lepers.
